templates.text: {echoText}. The idea hits the air like a bell. Ideas are louder than swords down here.
The ancients sealed the concept of {echoText} behind three locks. You just turned one.
{echoText}. You feel it reorganize something behind your eyes. The dungeon noticed.
Every wall in this corridor is covered in attempts to describe {echoText}. None of them got it right. Until now.
{echoText} is older than these walls. The walls were built to contain it. They have been failing slowly for centuries.
Something in the deep has known about {echoText} since before the concept had a name. It is unsurprised by your answer. It has been waiting for you to catch up.
The dungeon considers {echoText}. It has its own interpretation. The two versions do not entirely agree.
The dust settles differently after {echoText}. Subtle — only noticeable if you know what to look for.
{echoText}. The stone absorbs it. Everything spoken in the dungeon is absorbed. That is why the walls know things.
Carved somewhere in the lower levels: {echoText}. Carved before anyone could speak it. The dungeon anticipated this.
The archive updates. {echoText} — filed, cross-referenced, stored alongside the other things you know. The collection grows.
Every seeker who descended carrying {echoText} changed the dungeon slightly. You are the latest change.
